text: As artificial intelligence becomes more common in daily life, questions about ethics have become very important. One major concern is bias. If an AI system is trained on data that contains unfair human prejudices, it may produce biased outcomes, for example, in hiring or loan approvals. This is why fairness must be a priority—AI should treat everyone equally. Privacy is another key issue. AI systems often collect large amounts of personal data, so people need to know how their information is being used. That’s where transparency comes in: companies should explain how their AI works and makes decisions. Overall, responsible AI development means building technology that is fair, clear, and respectful of people’s rights.
